headline: MACC's corruption perception survey suppressed for second consecutive year
context: MACC's annual National Corruption Barometer survey results were not published for the second year running, with officials citing 'methodology review' despite using the same methodology since 2018.

cards.big: MACC's corruption survey suppressed 2 years in a row — what are the results showing?
cards.sub: Annual barometer measuring public corruption perception withheld without explanation

cards.big: MACC Act Section 7(c) mandates public education and information on corruption
cards.sub: Suppressing the survey arguably violates MACC's own statutory information mandate

cards.big: Survey costs RM2.8 million annually to conduct with 15,000 respondents nationwide
cards.sub: Public money spent collecting data that the public is not allowed to see

cards.big: Last published barometer in 2023 showed corruption perception worsening in 4 of 6 sectors
cards.sub: Suppression followed a survey that produced politically inconvenient findings

cards.big: An anti-corruption agency that hides its own corruption survey has become part of the problem
Should MACC survey results be automatically published within 30 days of completion?
